questions.question: Multiply $\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0 \\ 0 & 2 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 3 \\ 4 \end{bmatrix}$. What are the new coordinates?
Apply the reflection matrix $\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{bmatrix}$ to the point $(5, -3)$.
questions.explanation: $x$ stays $5$, $y$ changes sign: $-(-3)=3$. Reflection across the $x$-axis gives $(5,3)$.

questions.question: What happens to a geometric shape when its vertex matrix is multiplied by the identity matrix?
options: The shape is reflected across the origin.
questions.explanation: The identity matrix acts as the number $1$; any matrix multiplied by it remains the same.

questions.question: Rotate the point $(2, 5)$ by $90^{\circ}$ counterclockwise using $\begin{bmatrix} 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$.
If a triangle has vertices at $(1, 1), (3, 1)$, and $(1, 4)$, what are the dimensions of its vertex matrix?
questions.explanation: The vertex matrix has $2$ rows (for $x$ and $y$ coordinates) and $3$ columns (one for each vertex).

questions.question: Reflect the point $(-3, 7)$ across the $y$-axis using $\begin{bmatrix} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$.
Apply the shear transformation $\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$ to the point $(3, 1)$.
If you apply the matrix $\begin{bmatrix} 0.5 & 0 \\ 0 & 0.5 \end{bmatrix}$ to a square with an area of 4, what is the new area?
questions.explanation: Linear scale factor is $0.5$. Area scale factor is $(0.5)^2 = 0.25$. New area: $4 \times 0.25 = 1$.

questions.question: Which operation combines two transformations in sequence?
questions.explanation: Multiplying transformation matrices together creates a single matrix that performs both steps at once.

questions.question: Find the determinant of the reflection matrix $\begin{bmatrix} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$. What does it indicate?
questions.explanation: $\det = (-1)(1) - (0)(0) = -1$. Reflections have determinant $-1$ — they reverse orientation but preserve area.

scoreMessages.90: Great job! You understand how matrices transform points — dilation, reflection, rotation, shear, computing new coordinates, and analyzing area changes and orientation.
scoreMessages.70: Well done. Key reminders: (1) Dilation: multiply both coordinates by the scale factor, (2) Reflection across x-axis: negate y; across y-axis: negate x, (3) Rotation $90^{\circ}$ CCW: $(x,y) \to (-y,x)$, (4) Shear: shifts one coordinate by a multiple of the other.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Applying the transformation matrix by multiplying it with the coordinate matrix, (2) Area scale factor = square of the linear scale factor, (3) Determinant of $-1$ means orientation reversed, (4) Vertex matrix dimensions: rows = coordinates, columns = vertices.
scoreMessages.0: Review matrix transformations: (1) Multiply the transformation matrix by the coordinate matrix to get new coordinates, (2) Dilation: $\begin{bmatrix} k & 0 \\ 0 & k \end{bmatrix}$ scales by $k$, (3) Reflection across x-axis: $\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & -1 \end{bmatrix}$, across y-axis: $\begin{bmatrix} -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$, (4) Rotation $90^{\circ}$ CCW: $\begin{bmatrix} 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$.
